segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They returned to Thornwick as the sun climbed toward noon, walking through streets that would never be the same.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The transformation was everywhere -- glass-smooth walls that had been wood, crystalline puddles where water had boiled and frozen in the same instant, cobblestones fused into organic curves that caught light like gems.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: People stared as they passed. Not at Vex -- she drew glances, certainly, with her mismatched eyes and road-worn bearing -- but at Caelin. At his arm.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He'd rolled up his sleeve without thinking, too hot from the climb out of the crater.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Now the embedded scale was visible to anyone who looked: that thumb-length hex of dark obsidian inlaid flush with his skin, the hair-fine filaments threading away from its edges like black veins beneath the surface.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ember-motes drifted lazily in the scale's depths, visible even in daylight.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "They're afraid,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex murmured beside him.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I saved a child,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, but even to his own ears it sounded hollow.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You did. And then you went to meet a dragon and came back changed."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She kept her voice low, conversational.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "People fear what they don't understand. And that -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: a subtle gesture at his arm

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: " -- they definitely don't understand."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed warm. No sting. She was right, as usual.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They found Elder Gareth in the village square, directing a handful of survivors as they loaded a wagon with salvaged supplies. He looked up as they approached, his gaze going immediately to Caelin's exposed forearm.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The old man's eyes widened, then narrowed with something that might have been recognition.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "So,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth said quietly, straightening with effort.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "She marked you."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "You know what this is?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin asked, surprise and relief mixing in equal measure.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I'm old enough to remember stories my grandmother told. Stories her grandmother told her."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth stepped closer, studying the scale without touching.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "The dragon-marked. The ember-born. The old songs spoke of them -- those who carried fire in their blood and could be chosen as bearers for something greater."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: His voice dropped.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I didn't think I'd live to see it proven true."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "The dragon -- Vharisax -- she said I have to find something. Nine somethings. Relics, she called them. And gather others who can bear them."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The words tumbled out faster than intended.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "She said if I don't, something sealed beneath the mountains will wake. Something that shouldn't."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth was quiet for a long moment, his weathered face unreadable. Then he nodded slowly.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Then you'll go. You don't have a choice, do you?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin looked down at the scale.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "No. I really don't."

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "The mark won't let you,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth said with certainty.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "It'll pull you toward what needs doing. Push you when you hesitate. Drag you if you try to run."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He met Caelin's eyes.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "My grandmother said the dragon-marked were blessed and cursed in equal measure. The blessing: purpose absolute. The curse: no peace until that purpose is fulfilled."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "That's about right,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex muttered.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth turned his attention to her, assessing.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "And you are?"

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Someone following him because he's following something I need to find."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's honesty was almost aggressive.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "My mother died in those mountains looking for whatever he's bound to seek. I'm going to find out why."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The elder nodded as if this made perfect sense.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Then you'll watch his back while he watches the path. Good. He'll need someone practical."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He turned back to Caelin.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "When do you leave?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Today,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, though he hadn't consciously decided. The scale warmed in agreement.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "The path leads to the Emberpeaks. The sooner we follow it, the sooner..."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He trailed off, unsure how to finish.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "The sooner you can rest,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth finished for him.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "If you ever can."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He clasped Caelin's shoulder with surprising strength.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "You saved my granddaughter. That's a debt Thornwick won't forget. If you need supplies, take them. If you need silver, we have a little. If you need a blessing -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: his voice roughened

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: " -- you have mine. Go with purpose. Return with answers."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I'll try,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin managed.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "Don't try. Do."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth's grip tightened.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "The dragon chose you for a reason. Trust that, when everything else fails."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A small voice called out:

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "Caelin!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira broke away from a group of children and ran toward them, her soot-stained dress flapping. She skidded to a stop in front of him, eyes wide and bright.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "You're leaving,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said. Not a question.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "I have to."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Because of what the red lady gave you."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her gaze dropped to his arm, to the scale embedded there. She reached out one small hand, hesitated, then gently touched the obsidian surface with one fingertip.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed beneath her touch -- a single bright flare of light that made the ember-motes swirl faster. Mira gasped but didn't pull away.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "It's warm,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she whispered.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Like a heartbeat."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "It is, in a way,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, his throat tight.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira pulled her hand back and looked up at him with an intensity that seemed too old for her age.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "The rhyme. The one the dragon sang. You're going to find what it talks about, aren't you? The Nine?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Yes."

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "Will you come back?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The question hit harder than expected. The scale stayed steady -- it would let him answer honestly either way.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I don't know,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "The path is long. What I'm looking for is dangerous. But if I can..."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stopped, tried again.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I'll try."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale stung -- sharp and bright. He winced.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira's eyes widened.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "It hurt you. Why did it hurt you?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Because I wasn't being truthful enough."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He knelt so they were eye-level.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "Let me try again. I don't know if I'll come back. What I'm doing might take years. It might take me to places I can't return from. But -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: and this he knew was true, felt it bone-deep

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: " -- if I succeed, if I find what I'm meant to find, then places like Thornwick will be safer. Dragons won't come searching. Villages won't burn. Children won't lose their mothers."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale stayed warm. No sting. Truth accepted.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira nodded solemnly, then surprised him by wrapping her arms around his neck in a fierce hug.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "Then you have to succeed,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she whispered against his shoulder.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "You have to."

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "I will,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, and hoped it wasn't a lie.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When she pulled back, she was crying, but her chin was set with determination.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I'll remember the rhyme. Every word. And when you come back, I'll say it to you again so you know I kept it safe."

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "I'd like that."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth gently called Mira back, and she went reluctantly, looking over her shoulder until she disappeared into the group of children being loaded onto the wagon.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "They're evacuating to Millbrook,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth said.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "The village council there owes us a debt from the grain shortages five years back. We'll rebuild. Someday."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He looked around at the glass-transformed ruins.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "Or we'll build something new. Either way, we'll survive."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I'm sorry,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "If I'd come sooner -- "

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "You came when you could. You saved who you could."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The elder's voice was firm.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "That's all anyone can ask. The rest is on the dragon, and she's answered for it."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex touched Caelin's arm -- the unmarked one.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "We should go. Afternoon's burning, and I'd rather be in the foothills before dark. Less chance of running into whatever might be drawn by the dragon's death."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She was right. The scale pulsed agreement.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin looked around at Thornwick one last time -- at the survivors, the transformed buildings, the smoke still rising from deeper ruins. He'd arrived at dawn to find catastrophe. He was leaving at midday carrying a burden he barely understood.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But the flame-path stretched before him, bright and insistent and impossible to ignore.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "Take this,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth said, pressing a small pouch into Caelin's hand. It clinked with coin.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Not much, but enough to get you through the next few towns. And this -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He pulled a folded piece of cloth from his pocket, opened it to reveal a simple silver pendant on a leather cord.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "My wife's. She'd want you to have it. For luck."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I can't -- "

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "You can, and you will."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth closed Caelin's fingers around it.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Honor her memory by surviving whatever's ahead."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin nodded, unable to speak past the tightness in his throat.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They gathered what little they needed -- dried meat, water skins, a spare cloak for Vex -- and walked to the eastern edge of the village where the flame-path led. The thread of fire stretched across the fields toward where the land began its climb into the foothills.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Only Caelin could see it, but he felt its pull like a physical thing now, stronger with each passing moment. The scale pulsed in rhythm with it, urging him forward.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "So we just... walk toward the mountains?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex asked.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Following something only you can see?"

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "That's the plan."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Terrible plan."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But she adjusted her pack and checked her daggers.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I've followed worse."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They set out together, leaving Thornwick's smoke behind. The flame-path stretched before them like a thread connecting present to uncertain future.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin kept his marked arm exposed -- there was no point hiding it, and the scale seemed to pulse brighter when covered, as if objecting to concealment.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: As they walked, the embedded scale kept time with his heartbeat, a steady rhythm that was both foreign and intimate. The filaments beneath his skin had settled into their patterns, no longer feeling like invasion but like something that had always been there, waiting.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The Emberpeaks grew larger on the horizon, their slopes darkening from blue to grey as afternoon light shifted. Somewhere in those mountains waited the First Sanctum. Somewhere ahead lay the Dragon's Ember, first of the Nine Relics.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: And somewhere beyond that -- beyond the paths and purposes and ancient pacts -- waited the thing that must never wake.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But that was future's problem. Present's problem was putting one foot in front of the other, following a path of fire only he could see, trusting a scale embedded in his flesh to guide him true.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "So,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said after they'd walked in silence for a while,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "what happens when we actually reach this sanctum? Do you have a plan?"

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Find the relic. Don't die. Probably in that order."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "That's not a plan. That's barely even hope."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed warm. No sting. It was the truth.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "It's what I have,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Then I guess it'll have to be enough."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex squinted toward the mountains.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Though for the record, I still can't see anything except regular mountains and impending doom."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The doom's definitely there."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Fantastic."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But there was something almost like humor in her voice.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "At least we're doomed together. That's practically a friendship."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Despite everything -- the weight of the mark, the magnitude of the task, the uncertainty of what lay ahead -- Caelin found himself almost smiling.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The flame-path stretched onward. The scale pulsed steady. The mountains waited.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: And somewhere in the distance, something ancient stirred in its sleep, feeling the first link of the chain being forged anew.
